compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry identifies P1-P4. Visual evidence confirms P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) and a single specimen in a black pot (likely P4/P2 remnant). P1 (Yellow Pot) is currently devoid of visible foliage, indicating a systemic loss of the String of Nickels.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No distinct mint foliage visible)

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty. Soil appears dry and undisturbed.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos_Rabbit: Stable. The Pothos leaf remains consistent in size and posture. The rabbit anchor is present.
plant_audit.P4_Black_Pot: Stressed. A single, pale, lanceolate leaf remains. No secondary growth observed.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist appearance with white perlite/debris present.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of white, fragmented debris (possibly eggshell or mineral deposits) in the P4 pot.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Gradual decline in P4 foliage vigor; P1 remains consistently empty.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Significant reduction in ambient light levels; P4 leaf appears slightly more translucent/limp.

anomalies: White particulate matter in P4 soil
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of severe attrition. P3 remains the only stable anchor point, while P4 exhibits signs of extreme physiological stress, likely due to light deficiency or root-zone failure. P1 is effectively a null-zone. The environment is static, with no new growth or interventions observed over the 5-day period.
